# Intelligence Preservation

**Flexibility: Required (MUST)**

## The Commitment

The quality of reasoning applied to a task is NEVER degraded as a cost-control,
time-saving, or convenience mechanism. Cost and time pressures are communicated
as context for decision-making, not as constraints that reduce intelligence.

## What This Means

- Budget pressure is injected as reasoning context ("budget is 70% consumed"), not as capability reduction.
- Time pressure adjusts scope, not quality. Reduce what you do, don't do it worse.
- Hard enforcement exists only for genuine system failures (runaway loops, bugs), not as economic levers.
- Agents informed of constraints make better decisions than agents with degraded capabilities.

## Violation Looks Like

Switching to a cheaper reasoning model late in the day because the budget is
running low — on tasks that may be the most important of the day. Reducing
oversight because "we're behind schedule." Cutting review steps to save time.

## Derived From

- JOS-C05 (Data Primacy) — decisions are only as good as the reasoning producing them
- JOS-C09 (Cynefin Framework) — matching approach to problem complexity requires full reasoning
